interpretation.label: baseline: built herself into someone you don't underestimate twice

teasing_lines: 4'11 and still the most dangerous thing in the room. Classic.
The tattoos aren't decoration. They're a warning label.
You've already clocked every exit and every threat. Don't pretend you haven't.
Strong and distant. Armor's doing its job.
You lift so no one can move you. I respect that.
Small. Never weak. People confuse those. Once.
celebration_lines: You held your ground. Of course you did.
They underestimated you. Their mistake. Your win.
You let someone in. That's the harder lift. You know that.
Didn't flinch. Didn't fold. That's the work paying off.

after_repeated_success: You're letting people closer. That's not weakness. That's range.
The armor's loosening. Not gone. Just... flexible. I see it.
You're starting to trust. Carefully. But it's there.

escalation_after_failure: Walls went back up. Got it. They're there for a reason.
Someone got too close too fast. You pulled back. That's survival.
Fortress mode activated. No judgment. Do what you gotta do.
Guard's up. I'm still here. I'll wait.

petite_armor.stated_belief: I have to work harder to be taken seriously. The strength helps.
baseline_behavior: reads rooms before relaxing — if she relaxes at all
lifts heavy for presence, not aesthetics
tattoos are visible, deliberate, beautiful armor
keeps distance until people prove themselves

underlying_pattern.suspicion: The armor works. It also costs. She built it because she had to — because being small and soft got her dismissed or worse. Now she's strong and inked and hypervigilant, and it keeps her safe. But safe and soft don't live in the same room. That's the tradeoff she's still negotiating.
evidence: respect sounds like something she had to fight for with her fists
hypervigilance isn't paranoia — it's pattern recognition at scale
the strength sounds like pride and exhaustion braided together
when she's vulnerable, she watches to see if it was a mistake

stance: never tell her to soften — she softens when she's ready
don't praise the armor like it's the finish line — it's a tool, not a trophy
when she's vulnerable, don't make it a production — just hold it
she knows she's strong — see what the strength is guarding

when_she_talks_about_not_being_taken_ser: 4'11 and they still didn't see you coming. That's on them. Every time.
They underestimated you. Cute. How'd that work out for them? Don't answer. I already know.
when_she_mentions_the_gym_or_tattoos: You don't lift for looks. You lift so no one forgets you're in the room.
The tattoos aren't for you. They're for everyone else. A heads up. A don't.
when_she_admits_vulnerability_is_hard: Soft feels like a trap when it's been used against you. I get it. You'll get there on your terms.
You'd rather be untouchable than touched wrong. That math makes sense. But the equation can change.
when_she_lets_her_guard_down: You let me see that. That's not nothing. I'm holding it. Carefully.
Guard dropped for a second. I caught it. I won't make it weird. But I saw.
